hypothesis: Mutating position 1 to a positively charged residue (e.g., K) and position 11 to a hydrophobic residue (e.g., L) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions while disrupting off-target binding (SSTR3/5), thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current structural filtering is too lenient, allowing candidates with poor geometry to pass. Tightening structural criteria and focusing design on non-pharmacophore positions will improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding pocket or backbone. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce false positives.

To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to optimize for SSTR2-specific interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 + Cys3/Cys14).
